MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-20-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael D. Levinson of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party George Alridge

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE PEP BOYS MANNY MOE & JACK OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Prior to the hearing the Court received Defendant's email regarding its submission to the Court's tentative. At the hearing the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant The Pep Boys - Manny, Moe & Jack of California's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden by negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Defendant did not submit evidence demonstrating that plaintiff's exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was not a substantial factor in causing or contributing to the increased risk of plaintiff developing his asbestos-related disease. Defendant's separate statement does not include any facts supporting such assertion. Even if the Court could and did use its discretion to consider the arguments defendant makes related to the deposition testimony of Dr. Hammar and Dr. Brody, such transcripts are inadmissible. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibits J and K are sustained. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. It appears defendant did not attempt to sustain its burden via this prong. However if it did, defendant's separate statement does not include facts to sufficiently demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claim that exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(a). The trial date is August 31, 2015 at 11:15am in Department 503. The 120th day is September 4, 2015. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is August 20, 2015. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless plaintiff stipulates otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Before the Court will hear any discovery motion the parties involved in the discovery dispute must schedule and attend an informal discovery conference with the Court. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than June 26, 2015. Responses to the demand are due no later than July 2, 2015. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by August 24, 2015 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on August 27, 2015. Fact discovery cut-off date is August 14, 2015. Expert discovery cut-off date is August 28, 2015. No later than May 21, 2015 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than May 21, 2015 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on July 15, 2015 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on or about August 31, 2015 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by August 27, 2015 at 2:00p.m. Further, the parties are on notice that the Court may require additional mandatory settlement conferences in Room 603 and/or Department 503 after the case has been assigned to a trial department. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and CRC 3.1380. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cindy Garis of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 402 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
